hero.headline: Regenerative Sanctuary Portal
hero.tagline: Dwell with purpose. Live with consciousness.
hero.description: Join the community that chose Costa Rica as their sanctuary — living lands seeking conscious stewards.

home.section_description: Costa Rican sanctuaries under active protection, seeking conscious stewards.

division_pz.tagline: A biodiversity sanctuary where forest and water coexist in perfect harmony.

la_carpintera.tagline: The perfect duality between strategic connectivity and ecological isolation: a cloud forest sanctuary with absolute sovereignty in water and energy.

technical_sheet.title: Technical Specification
technical_sheet.disclaimer: The information presented is based on public data and technical analysis. For specific legal terms, consult official documents.

flora_fauna.title: Living Sanctuary
flora_fauna.subtitle: Flora & Fauna
flora_fauna.description: A complete ecosystem where flora and fauna coexist in perfect harmony.

ancestral_wisdom.title: Ancestral Wisdom
ancestral_wisdom.subtitle: Indigenous Knowledge
ancestral_wisdom.description: Respect and integration of ancestral knowledge with modern conservation.
ancestral_wisdom.memory_boruca: Boruca Memory · Guardians of the South

division_pz.name: División Pérez Zeledón
division_pz.tagline: Where primary forest is the water bank the world needs.
division_pz.description: Nearly 600 hectares of primary and secondary forest within the La Amistad Pacific South Biological Corridor, designated as a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
division_pz.location: División, Pérez Zeledón, San José, Costa Rica
division_pz.technical_area: División, Pérez Zeledón (San José). 594 hectares of unified nature reserve.
division_pz.technical_water: Primary water source of the División River. Active commercial water concession.
division_pz.technical_zoning: Located within the Biosphere Reserve (UNESCO Certification). 91% forest coverage acting as a carbon sink.
division_pz.technical_elevation: 1,800 to 2,800 meters above sea level. Exceptional transition between humid tropical forest and montane cloud forest.
division_pz.technical_biodiversity: Pristine habitat with megafauna presence: pumas and tapirs (jaguars). Resplendent quetzal nesting zone.
division_pz.technical_potential: Limited construction potential to 5 hectares with maximum density of 1 dwelling per 10 hectares under sustainability restrictions.
division_pz.technical_privacy: Sole access via private road. Natural acoustic isolation due to mountainous topography.
division_pz.technical_logistics: Access via Route 237 (30 minutes to Uvita). Basic services: private spring water, installed solar energy, satellite communication.

la_carpintera.name: La Carpintera
la_carpintera.tagline: The perfect duality between strategic connectivity and ecological isolation.
la_carpintera.description: Two estates in San Rafael de La Unión, Cartago. Estate 1 (10.96 ha) with strategic connectivity and development potential. Estate 2 (2.78 ha) with pristine regenerated forest.
la_carpintera.location: San Rafael de La Unión, Cartago, Costa Rica
la_carpintera.technical_area: 10.96 ha (109,615.34 m²) in San Rafael de La Unión, Cartago. Strategic conservation property in critical zone for Central Valley.
la_carpintera.technical_water: Own water spring unregistered in high zone. Aqueduct easement (municipal tank). MINAE Permit Exp. 7479-A. High water charge zone for Central Valley.
la_carpintera.technical_zoning: Cerros de la Carpintera Protected Zone — SINAC/MINAE. Water and Biodiversity Conservation
la_carpintera.technical_elevation: 1,550–1,800 msnm. Unmatched transition toward Lower Montane Very Humid Forest ecosystem.
la_carpintera.technical_biodiversity: Pristine biological sanctuary. Vital biological corridor with 250+ bird species (quetzals, goldfinches, green toucans). Pumas, two-toed sloths. Endemic flora (orchids, tree ferns). Active carbon sink.
la_carpintera.technical_potential: 60% open area allows planning and construction of infrastructure up to 10% of land. Ideal for training centers, low-density residences, laboratories.
la_carpintera.technical_privacy: High topographic security with protected environment. Land use strictly regulated by MINAE and SINAC for 'Water and Biodiversity Conservation', guaranteeing absolute ecological isolation.
la_carpintera.technical_logistics: Paved road to 400m before gate. Final 400m gravel (4x4). Efficient logistics: 2 km from San Rafael, 12 km from Cartago, 22 km from San José.

la_carpintera_finca2.technical_area: 2.78 ha (27,848.74 m²) in San Rafael de La Unión, Cartago. Natural reserve for low-impact development.
la_carpintera_finca2.technical_water: Spring water with own treatment. Own spring in low zone. Legally guaranteed aqueduct easement linked to Estate 1 (MINAE Exp. 7479-A).
la_carpintera_finca2.technical_zoning: Cerros de la Carpintera Protected Zone — SINAC/MINAE. Fully integrated for Water and Biodiversity Conservation.
la_carpintera_finca2.technical_elevation: 1,650–1,720 msnm. Lower Montane Very Humid Forest. Constant 14–24°C, annual rainfall 2,000–2,500 mm.
la_carpintera_finca2.technical_biodiversity: Living ecological laboratory and refuge for pumas, sloths, orchids, and white oak. 250+ bird species. Essential biological corridor of maximum density and high water charge.
la_carpintera_finca2.technical_potential: Ideal for wellness projects. 10+ years free of agrochemicals creates unmatched soil chemical purity. Eligible for Organic Agriculture or Biodiversity Sanctuary certification.
la_carpintera_finca2.technical_privacy: Natural fortress guarded since 2016 against illegal logging and hunting. Very high forest density creates atmosphere of silence, deep peace, and total isolation.
la_carpintera_finca2.technical_logistics: Paved road with final 400m on private gravel road (4x4). 10 minutes from San Rafael, less than 1 hour from Juan Santamaría International Airport.

infrastructure.division_pz: Available services: Electricity, Internet, Telephone. Operational property.
infrastructure.la_carpintera: Solar panels, Starlink, excellent cellular coverage, drinking water. Two residential units and storage buildings.
infrastructure.la_carpintera_finca2: Carbon-zero infrastructure: 100% solar energy, organic nursery free of agrochemicals, Starlink, good cellular coverage, spring water, wildlife support ecosystem improvements.

timeline.title: Boruca-Brunca Territory
timeline.description: The Boruca (Brunca) peoples establish their civilization in the División River basin. The land is understood as a living being with memory and sacred purpose.

timeline.title: Colonial Contact
timeline.description: Spanish conquest alters the territorial dynamics of the Southern Zone. The Boruca resist tenaciously and preserve much of their worldview and language to this day.

timeline.title: Agricultural Colonization
timeline.description: Colonial families settle in Pérez Zeledón. Coffee, sugar cane and cattle ranching begin to transform the landscape, though high zones maintain their forest cover.

timeline.title: Conservation Policies
timeline.description: Costa Rica establishes a pioneering conservation legal framework in Latin America. The La Amistad Biological Corridor is established, protecting El Robledal territory.

timeline.title: UNESCO Recognition
timeline.description: The La Amistad Pacífico Sur Conservation Area is confirmed as a World Heritage Site. El Robledal, within the corridor, acquires international conservation status.

timeline.title: In Search of Custodians
timeline.description: Nearly 600 hectares of primary forest seek a conscious custodian. A life project that honors the World Heritage that this land represents.

timeline.title: Huetar Routes
timeline.description: Mystical and sacred zone: Ancient indigenous commercial and spiritual route. An environment considered sacred for its purity and exceptional natural capacity to generate life through water.

timeline.title: Traditional Land Use
timeline.description: Decades of traditional family land use. Estate 1 was dedicated to cattle ranching and Estate 2 to coffee and goat dairy; activities that heavily compacted soil and introduced contaminants.

timeline.title: Vision of Transformation
timeline.description: The "Para Siempre Amigos de La Carpintera" organization acquires the land assuming a high voluntary financial cost. The objective? To radically halt degradation and convert former pastures into a unified cloud forest sanctuary.

timeline.title: Operations Closure and Regeneration
timeline.description: All cattle ranching, grazing and agrochemical use stopped abruptly. The soil immediately regains its water infiltration capacity and native pioneer tree species spontaneously emerge.

timeline.title: Restoration and Active Stewardship
timeline.description: Owners relocate permanently to the site in 2016 to personally halt illegal hunting and logging. Consistent with their ecological footprint, they completely forgo the commercial electrical grid and install an autonomous system of solar panels, organic nurseries and beekeeping ponds.

timeline.title: Absolute Operational Sovereignty
timeline.description: Starlink satellite internet is implemented and the estates are unified through a strategic aqueduct easement. The complex achieves 100% sovereignty of pure water by gravity and clean energy. Forest cover reaches 40% on Estate 1 and an impressive 85%–90% on Estate 2.

timeline.title: Mature Ecosystem and Global Legacy
timeline.description: Full compliance with SINAC Management Plan. The land is now a living laboratory serving as refuge for pumas, sloths and over 250 bird species, including the mythical Resplendent Quetzal. A mature asset, protected by the State and ready to receive an institutional custodian on an international scale.

prosperity.description: Strategic location within the UNESCO Biological Corridor, its own water volume and access from the Inter-American Highway make El Robledal a high-value asset. Its potential in ecotourism, organic production (active blackberries and avocado) and environmental services is immediate.

indicators.label: Ecotourism Potential
indicators.note: Direct access to biological corridors and active sustainable tourism certifications in the region.

indicators.label: Active Organic Production
indicators.note: Plots of cacao, banana and Boruca medicinal plants in production without agrochemicals.

indicators.label: Environmental Services Payment
indicators.note: Registered in FONAFIFO's Environmental Services Payment program — annual income guaranteed by forest conservation.

indicators.label: UNESCO Corridor Appreciation
indicators.note: Borders UNESCO Biosphere Reserve Core Zone — exceptional patrimonial appreciation.

indicators.label: Community Impact
indicators.note: Alliances with Boruca and Bribri communities for shared cultural and agricultural management.

issues.problem: Unemployment in Pérez Zeledón rural zones
issues.solution: Generation of green jobs: nature guides, organic farmers, local artisans integrated into the project.
Active biological corridors and reforestation with native species that increase World Heritage value.
Model of responsible water stewardship: private springs supply neighboring communities under collaboration agreement.

ancestral.title: Boruca Memory — Guardians of the South
ancestral.description: The Boruca (Brunca) nation has inhabited these lands for millennia. Their worldview recognizes in every river, mountain and tree a living being with memory and purpose. The Festival of the Little Devils, celebrated annually near these lands, is the oldest cultural resistance in Central America.

lifestyle: Awake with cloud forest chorus — transition of life zones between 1,800 and 2,800 msnm
Eternal spring climate: 15 to 22°C year-round without mechanical air conditioning
Hikes on 7 km of internal trails in primary forest — direct access to División River springs
Certified water from private spring, captured from mountain water torrents
At 2 km from División community · 30 km (45 min) from San Gerardo de Dota · 35 km (45 min) from San Isidro
Starlit skies without light pollution in one of Central America's cleanest basins (UNESCO)
Operational sovereignty: direct access from paved Inter-American Route + complete service infrastructure

potencial.title: Integrated Wellness
potencial.desc: El Robledal offers a superlative scenario for comprehensive health. Spanning elevations from 1,800 to 2,800 msnm, it guarantees perpetual comfort climate. Its high-mountain air and complete water sovereignty (owning the very source of División River) create a pristine environment, ideal for establishing isolated sanctuaries.

potencial.title: Environmental Conservation
potencial.desc: Mega-asset of conservation with 594 hectares and 91% forest cover, functioning as critical anchor for Biological Corridor connectivity toward the Central Pacific. Its location within UNESCO Biosphere Reserve territory and presence of megafauna like tapirs and pumas grant it unmatched international validation apart from its important water resources.

potencial.title: Education and Research
potencial.desc: Houses within a single property the transition of all Holdridge Life Zones, converting it into a real-scale ecological laboratory. It is the ideal location to establish an international university station focused on watershed management and climate change.

potencial.title: Regenerative Tourism
potencial.desc: Unlike other reserves with total restriction, El Robledal's land use allows low-density construction (dwellings every 10,000 m²). Leveraging its 7 km of internal roads and paved access, the buyer has the perfect canvas to develop a sustainable luxury eco-lodge.

potencial.title: Scientific Research
potencial.desc: The estate is the cradle of División River and key tributary of Savegre River. Monitoring tapir, feline and bird populations across these 600 hectares represents an inexhaustible opportunity to attract grants and high-level scientific tourism.

potencial.title: Spiritual Development
potencial.desc: The isolation provided by nearly 600 hectares, combined with the constant energy of pure water torrents, generates the atmosphere of silence and absolute isolation demanded by the world's most exclusive retreat projects.

potencial.title: Community Integration
potencial.desc: Establishing an institutional project here will mitigate migration and local poverty by halting deforestation and poaching through generation of stable green jobs (forest guardians, guides, hospitality).

descriptions.amor: Protecting 594 hectares of forest is the greatest act of love and commitment toward our planet. This sanctuary, backed by UNESCO certification, safeguards in perpetuity the pristine habitat of quetzals, pumas and tapirs, guaranteeing that the mountain's heartbeat is preserved intact for future generations.
descriptions.salud: An environment sculpted by nature for physical restoration and mental clarity. The unbreakable purity of cloud forest air, an eternal spring climate (15°C - 22°C) and absolute control over a private crystalline water spring offer perfect conditions for an absolute wellness lifestyle and healing.
descriptions.sabiduria: This region, guarded for millennia by the indigenous Boruca nation, possesses profound spiritual and historical dimension. Here, true wisdom is achieved by immersing oneself in the peace of its multiple ecosystems, learning from primary forest resilience and reconnecting with the sacred and silent purpose of the land.
descriptions.prosperidad: True modern wealth resides in autonomy and natural capital safeguarding. With direct access, operational infrastructure and confirmed legal viability for minimum-impact development, the property represents an incomparable trophy asset that guarantees peace, radical privacy and long-term security.

legacy.title: An Intergenerational Legacy
legacy.message: Inherited from grandfather and consolidated by the Ortiz brothers, El Robledal was purchased wholly to protect the integrity of 594 hectares. The sons of current owners have other life projects and wish to pass stewardship to an organization, NGO, foundation or individual who understands the macro-ecological value of this mega-reserve. Whoever receives it becomes guardian of División River's spring, custodian of a World Heritage Site, protector of native megafauna and guarantor of pure water for future generations. This is the call of the La Amistad Pacífico Sur Biological Corridor.

quetzal-pz.name: Resplendent Quetzal
quetzal-pz.description: Sacred bird of Mesoamerica. Documented in El Robledal as a permanent nester in the highest elevation zones (2,000+ msnm). Its presence confirms forest maturity with abundant wild avocadillos.
quetzal-pz.habitat: Cloud forest and mountain forest, 1,500–3,000 msnm

tapir-danta.name: Tapir (Baird's Tapir)
tapir-danta.description: Megafauna characteristic of the La Amistad Biological Corridor. El Robledal offers optimal habitat as a seed disperser in primary forest zones at high elevation.
tapir-danta.habitat: Tropical primary and secondary forest, 1,200–3,000 msnm

coyote.name: Coyote
coyote.description: Key predator of El Robledal's ecosystem. Present in primary forest zones as a regulator of small mammal populations.

halcon-rojo.name: Red-tailed Hawk
halcon-rojo.description: Native raptor of the Biological Corridor. Documented in El Robledal as an indicator of pristine ecosystem. Patrols open forest zones in search of small prey.
halcon-rojo.habitat: Cloud forest and montane forest, 1,200–3,000 msnm

cabro-monte.name: White-tailed Deer
cabro-monte.description: Herbivorous megafauna documented in El Robledal. Present in primary forest zones as an indicator of ecosystem with low hunting pressure. Key seed disperser.

puma-pz.name: Puma (Mountain Lion)
puma-pz.description: Top predator of the La Amistad Biological Corridor. Documented in El Robledal as critical ecological regulator. Its presence indicates an ecosystem in excellent conservation status.
puma-pz.habitat: Tropical primary and secondary forest, 1,200–3,000 msnm

zorro-pz.name: Gray Fox
zorro-pz.description: Medium-sized carnivore of El Robledal's ecosystem. Predator of small mammals and fruits that contributes to the ecological balance of primary forest.

perezoso-dos-dedos.name: Hoffmann's Two-toed Sloth
perezoso-dos-dedos.description: Arboreal mammal specialized in El Robledal's forest cover. Its presence in primary forest zones indicates ecosystem stability and maturity.

conejo-pz.name: Forest Rabbit
conejo-pz.description: Small herbivorous mammal of the Biological Corridor. Documented in El Robledal as a key food source for medium-sized predators and raptors.

liebre-pz.name: Jack Rabbit
liebre-pz.description: Jumping mammal present in open zones of secondary forest. Indicator of areas with forest cover mosaic in El Robledal.

murcielago-pz.name: Jamaican Fruit Bat
murcielago-pz.description: Chiropteran seed disperser crucial to El Robledal. Its nocturnal activity facilitates natural regeneration of primary forest.

trogon-pz.name: Violaceous Trogon
trogon-pz.description: Colorful bird documented in El Robledal. Inhabits mature cloud forest zones at the highest elevations of the property.
trogon-pz.habitat: Cloud forest and montane forest, 2,000–3,000 msnm

passiflora-gilbertiana.name: Gilbertian Passionfruit
passiflora-gilbertiana.description: Endemic species of Costa Rica documented in El Robledal. One of 71 endemic plant species identified in the Savegre Biosphere Reserve. Plant of high conservation value.

aguacatillo-pz.name: Avocadillo
aguacatillo-pz.description: Canopy tree characteristic of El Robledal's cloud forest. Preferred food of the Resplendent Quetzal. Its abundance in the property ensures permanent nesting of sacred birds.

roble-pz.name: Costa Rican Oak
roble-pz.description: Emblematic tree that gives El Robledal its name. Master species of montane forest. Its presence indicates mature and stable primary forest in the highest elevation zones.

helecho-arborescente.name: Tree Fern
helecho-arborescente.description: Understory structurer of cloud forest in El Robledal. Creates crucial moist microhabitats for invertebrate fauna and amphibians. Indicator of extreme humidity and pristine conservation.
helecho-arborescente.habitat: Very wet mountain cloud forest, 1,800–3,000 msnm

orquídea-pz.name: Cloud Forest Orchids
orquídea-pz.description: Family of epiphytic plants adorning the cloud forest canopy. Hundreds of species documented in the Biological Corridor. Their presence indicates high humidity and exceptional forest conservation.

musgo-liquen-pz.name: Cloud Forest Mosses and Lichens
musgo-liquen-pz.description: Key components of aerial biomass in El Robledal. Absorb moisture and nutrients from perpetual fog. Indicators of conservation status and mountain air purity.
musgo-liquen-pz.habitat: Very wet mountain cloud forest, 1,800–3,000 msnm

mora-pz.name: Blackberry
mora-pz.description: Productive fruit shrub cultivated in designated zones of El Robledal. Wildlife food and agroforestry product. Coexists with strict primary forest conservation.
mora-pz.habitat: Productive areas of montane forest, 1,800–2,400 msnm

aguacate-pz.name: Avocado
aguacate-pz.description: Agroforestry crop integrated into productive zones of El Robledal. Maintained under sustainability principles. Complements primary forest conservation at a 594-hectare scale.
aguacate-pz.habitat: Productive areas of montane forest, 1,800–2,600 msnm

prosperity.description: Absolute ecological protection with 10 years of active stewardship that eliminated illegal hunting and logging. With 85-90% forest density, it functions as a carbon sink and biological refuge free of agrochemicals. Active beekeeping, fauna ponds and 100% solar operation demonstrate that inhabiting the ecosystem protects it.

indicators.label: Forest Cover
indicators.note: 85-90% dense montane cloud forest (85-90% pure forest). Active carbon sink and biological corridor of maximum density.

indicators.label: Environmental Purity
indicators.note: 12 years free of agrochemicals. Active beekeeping, certified organic nursery and native fruit trees as biological indicators of exceptional quality.

indicators.label: Turnkey
indicators.note: Operational house, solar panels, Starlink, fauna ponds and beekeeping installed. Functional water system by gravity from spring.

indicators.label: No Legal Encumbrances
indicators.note: Without mortgages or liens. Active Property Tax exemption. Eligible for FONAFIFO Environmental Services Payment.

indicators.label: Operations Hub
indicators.note: Due to its compact size and high forest density, it functions as the perfect administrative headquarters for a larger conservation project.

issues.solution: Aqueduct easement legally bound to Estate 1 concession. Supply guaranteed by legal instrument independent of owner.
Specific technical replacement of battery bank. Opportunity to modernize system to latest generation with immediate return.
House operable from day one. Preventive maintenance and adjustment of interior spaces to buyer's technical requirements (laboratories or offices).

ancestral.title: Huetar Memory and Earth's Legacy
ancestral.description: Huetar commercial and spiritual route transformed from cattle ranching to ecosystem regeneration and low-impact agroturismo that safeguards the purity and sacred water cycle of the mountain. — HUETAR MEMORY · LA CARPINTERA

architecture.name: Infrastructure of Sovereignty
architecture.description: Solar panels, Starlink and private water replaced conventional networks. Turnkey infrastructure: two residential units, storage facilities and concrete access with zero carbon footprint.

architecture.name: Forest as Architecture
architecture.description: MINAE/SINAC Management Plan protects perpetual conservation. Recovering forest (40%) is vital corridor for pumas, sloths and 250+ birds. Natural terraces trace the original design.

lifestyle: Awakening at 1,500 msnm — mist embraces the oak forest and cold is the day's first greeting
250+ registered bird species — the dawn chorus at La Carpintera is a symphony without sheet music
22 km from San José, 12 km from Cartago: the best of two worlds in less than 45 minutes
Temperature of 15 to 21°C year-round — no air conditioning, no heating
Starlink internet and solar energy: global connectivity with total operational sovereignty
San Rafael de La Unión just 2 km away — coffee farming communities with centuries of tradition
Private trails in cloud forest: the biodiversity of a Conservation Area as your backyard

potencial.title: Integrated Wellness
potencial.desc: The properties in the La Carpintera Hills offer an unmatched refuge for comprehensive health. Their elevation provides a cool microclimate, pure cloud forest air and a natural barrier against pollution, creating a pristine and secure environment minutes from the city; the ideal setting to establish wellness sanctuaries founded on the pillar of health.

potencial.title: Environmental Conservation
potencial.desc: This mega-asset acts as a fundamental bastion for the La Carpintera Hills Protected Zone. Its forest cover guarantees essential biological connectivity of the Central Valley, protecting vital water sources and serving as a safe refuge for endemic species, giving it incalculable conservation value.

potencial.title: Education and Research
potencial.desc: Its geographic position as a natural climate filter in the valley converts it into a living ecological laboratory. It is the ideal location to establish an academic station or research center focused on cloud forest resilience and urban ecology, actively fostering the pillar of wisdom.

potencial.title: Regenerative Tourism
potencial.desc: Its strategic value lies in unmatched accessibility combined with absolute natural isolation. Land use and topography provide the perfect canvas for developing a low-density eco-lodge or volunteer programs where prosperity is achieved through a sustainable and highly exclusive model.

potencial.title: Scientific Research
potencial.desc: The richness of its ecosystems and its function as a green lung represent an inexhaustible opportunity. Monitoring the flora, birdlife and mammals of this biological corridor is a key attraction for forging academic alliances, securing international funding (grants) and promoting high-level scientific tourism.

potencial.title: Spiritual Development
potencial.desc: The atmosphere of the cloud forest, wrapped in mist and profound silence, combined with the high topographic security of the estates, generates an energy of absolute isolation. It is the perfect atmosphere of peace demanded by the world's most exclusive retreat projects, directly cultivating the pillar of love and inner connection.

potencial.title: Community Integration
potencial.desc: Developing an institutional and integrated project on these estates will mitigate urban pressure on the mountain, stopping deforestation by generating stable green jobs (forest guardians, nature guides, hospitality) and spreading prosperity and environmental education to surrounding communities.

descriptions.amor: Protecting 21.9 hectares of cloud forest is an act of love and commitment toward the future. This sanctuary, integrated into the UNESCO Biosphere Reserve and under SINAC protection, safeguards the habitat of pumas, sloths and 250+ bird species, ensuring the coming generation inherits a living heritage.
descriptions.salud: An unmatched refuge for comprehensive health at 1,500-1,800 msnm. Cool microclimate, pure cloud forest air, year-round temperature of 15 to 21°C without artificial systems, certified spring water and solar energy create perfect conditions for absolute wellness.
descriptions.sabiduria: Ancestral Huetar land transformed by the vision and radical stewardship of its owners. Here, true wisdom emerges from daily learning about forest resilience, natural water cycles and reconnection with the sacred purpose of nature.
descriptions.prosperidad: Prosperity without dependence. With total operational sovereignty (100% solar energy, spring water, satellite internet), perpetual legal protection (ZPCC, FONAFIFO), and strategic location 22 km from San José, the property represents intergenerational security and value that grows with conservation.

legacy.title: A Platform Ready for the Next Cycle
legacy.message: This property transformed from coffee plantation and goat dairy to dense cloud forest thanks to 12 years of active stewardship and natural regeneration without chemical intervention. Today it is the purest conservation asset in the corridor. The owner offers complete technical accompaniment during transition, with deep knowledge of the water ecosystem, biodiversity and installed energy sovereignty infrastructure. This estate awaits someone who understands that conserving is the most intelligent way to invest.

quetzal-carpintera.name: Resplendent Quetzal
quetzal-carpintera.description: Sacred bird of Mesoamerica. Indicator of mature forest. Present seasonally in La Carpintera between 1,550–1,800 msnm.

jilguero.name: Black-capped Nightingale-Thrush
jilguero.description: Endemic species of Costa Rica's highlands. Nocturnal cantor of the cloud forest canopy. Documented in La Carpintera as permanent resident.

1.name: White Oak
1.description: Majestic tree of great stature, characterized by robust trunk and dense crown. Key species of montane forest providing food and shelter to countless species. Its wood holds great cultural value in Costa Rica.

3.name: Spanish Cedar
3.description: Fast-growing tree with aromatic and high-value wood. Historic in Costa Rica for its cultural and economic importance. Restorer species that regenerates rapidly after disturbances.
3.habitat: Humid and very humid montane forest, 800–2,200 msnm

4.name: Strangler Fig
4.description: Fig tree with expansive crowns that harbor complete fauna ecosystems. Fruit producer year-round, essential food for birds, mammals and reptiles. Very important species in biological corridors.
4.habitat: Tropical and humid montane forest, 200–2,400 msnm

6.name: Vaquita Orchid
6.description: Epiphytic orchid with pendant inflorescences and aromatic flowers. Species dependent on stable moisture and temperature conditions of montane forest. Symbol of cloud forest ecological health.
6.habitat: Lower and middle montane cloud forest, 1,400–2,200 msnm

7.name: Lagartillo
7.description: Very common forest species in Costa Rica, belonging to the Rutaceae family (same as citrus). Characterized by its bark covered in large conical spines and very resistant wood. Aromatic tree of small to medium stature with compound leaves and capsular fruits. Ancestrally used in traditional Costa Rican medicine. Food source for specialized wildlife.
7.habitat: Humid and deciduous montane forest, 1,000–2,100 msnm

9.name: Tree Fern
9.description: Ancient tree fern whose lineage traces back to past geological eras. Creates specialized ecosystems around its fibrous trunk. Critical indicator of high-altitude cloud forests without human intervention.
9.habitat: Very humid montane cloud forest, 1,800–3,000 msnm

atlapete-matorrales.name: Chestnut-capped Brush-Finch
atlapete-matorrales.description: Understory species of montane forest. Documented in La Carpintera in low-density scrub and forest edges.

azuleja.name: Blue Tanager
azuleja.description: Bright blue tanager and highly visible. Common in canopy and understory of La Carpintera.

carpintero-hoffmann.name: Hoffmann's Woodpecker
carpintero-hoffmann.description: Active woodpecker of the canopy that generates nests used by other species. Indicator of mature forest health in La Carpintera.

chorlo-collares.name: Killdeer
chorlo-collares.description: Territorial and noisy plover. Documented in open areas of La Carpintera.
chorlo-collares.habitat: Open areas and forest edges, 0–2,500 msnm

clorofonia.name: Blue-hooded Euphonia
clorofonia.description: Small canopy tanager specialized in mistletoe fruits. Endemic to humid montane forests of Central America.

colibri-azul.name: Blue-throated Hummingbird
colibri-azul.description: Small metallic blue hummingbird. Visitor to the understory of La Carpintera feeding on native flowers.

colibri-centelleante.name: Scintillant Hummingbird
colibri-centelleante.description: The smallest hummingbird in the country. Specialist of low flowers in edges and clearings of La Carpintera's cloud forest.
colibri-centelleante.habitat: Cloud forest and high-altitude scrub, 1,200–3,000 msnm

colibri-magnifico.name: Magnificent Hummingbird
colibri-magnifico.description: One of the largest hummingbirds in the area. Attracted by tubular flower blooms in La Carpintera's cloud forest understory.

colibri-montanes.name: Purple-throated Mountain-gem
colibri-montanes.description: Key pollinator in middle and lower strata. Specialist species of Gesneriaceae and Loranthaceae flowers in La Carpintera.

colibri-rabirrufo.name: Rufous-tailed Hummingbird
colibri-rabirrufo.description: Active and territorial hummingbird of the understory. Key pollinator of high-altitude flowers in La Carpintera.

cucarachero-comun.name: House Wren
cucarachero-comun.description: Small and territorial bird known for its loud song. Present in La Carpintera in forest edges and open areas.

elaenia-copetona.name: Yellow-bellied Elaenia
elaenia-copetona.description: Small flycatcher of the canopy. Documented in La Carpintera as resident of the high-altitude zone.

gavilan-colifajeado.name: Zone-tailed Hawk
gavilan-colifajeado.description: Medium-sized raptor hunting reptiles and small mammals. Present in edges and clearings of La Carpintera.

gavilán-caminero.name: Roadside Hawk
gavilán-caminero.description: Small and active raptor hunting reptiles and small mammals. Present in edges and clearings of La Carpintera.

loro-perrito.name: Red-fronted Parrot
loro-perrito.description: Small endemic high-altitude parrot. Documented in La Carpintera feeding in native fruit trees of the forest.

mirlo-montana.name: Mountain Thrush
mirlo-montana.description: Mountain relative of the national Clay-colored Thrush, strictly adapted to the cool climate of Cartago's hills. Permanent resident of La Carpintera.

perico-frentirrojo.name: Red-fronted Parrakeet
perico-frentirrojo.description: Social and noisy parrot of the canopy. Documented in La Carpintera foraging on native fruit trees.

soterrey-chinchiriqui.name: Modest Wren
soterrey-chinchiriqui.description: Small insectivorous bird of shrubby habitat. Common in the tacotales and secondary forest edges of La Carpintera.

tangara-dorada.name: Silver-throated Tanager
tangara-dorada.description: Gregarious frugivore of canopy and understory. Important seed disperser of Melastomataceae in La Carpintera.

trogon-collarejo.name: Collared Trogon
trogon-collarejo.description: Territorial and loyal bird of the canopy. Documented in La Carpintera as permanent resident of mature forest.

trogon-dorado.name: Golden-headed Quetzal
trogon-dorado.description: Canopy trogon with distinctive golden plumage. Present in La Carpintera as permanent resident of mature forest.

tucancillo.name: Emerald Toucanet
tucancillo.description: Vital high-altitude resident for seed dispersal. Present in the canopy of La Carpintera foraging on Lauraceae and Melastomataceae.

yigvirro.name: Clay-colored Thrush
yigvirro.description: National bird of Costa Rica. Permanent resident throughout La Carpintera, documented in canopy and understory.

finca_1.title: Huetar Memory and Earth's Legacy
finca_1.description: Huetar commercial and spiritual route transformed from cattle ranching to ecosystem regeneration and low-impact agroturismo that safeguards the purity and sacred water cycle of the mountain. — HUETAR MEMORY · LA CARPINTERA

architecture.name: Infrastructure of Sovereignty
architecture.description: Solar panels, Starlink and private water replaced conventional networks. Turnkey infrastructure: two residential units, storage facilities and concrete access with zero carbon footprint.

architecture.name: Forest as Architecture
architecture.description: MINAE/SINAC Management Plan protects perpetual conservation. Recovering forest (40%) is vital corridor for pumas, sloths and 250+ birds. Natural terraces trace the original design.

finca_2.title: Huetar Memory and Coffee Heritage
finca_2.description: Huetar heritage transformed from coffee plantation and dairy to voluntary forest restoration free of agrochemicals for over a decade, healing soils and recovering the water cycle. — HUETAR MEMORY · LA CARPINTERA

architecture.name: Infrastructure of Sovereignty
architecture.description: Solar panels, Starlink and spring water by gravity guarantee zero-carbon self-sufficiency. Infrastructure: house, organic nursery and beekeeping ponds.

architecture.name: Forest as Architecture
architecture.description: Management Plan legally protects against urban expansion. Dense forest (85-90%) is the main asset; appreciation trails and fauna ponds are the living architecture of nature.
